text:
create a query to find DeletedPerson with this condition:
Look at recently created DeletedPerson records (say last 36 hours). the project was in rails 
To create a query to find `DeletedPerson` records that were created in the last 36 hours in a Rails application, you can use ActiveRecord's query interface. Assuming you have a `DeletedPerson` model, you can write a query like this:

```ruby
# Assuming you are using Rails 5 or later
DeletedPerson.where('created_at >= ?', 36.hours.ago)
```

This query will fetch all `DeletedPerson` records that were created in the last 36 hours.

If you want to ensure that the query is executed correctly, you can also add some error handling or logging:

```ruby
begin
  recent_deleted_people = DeletedPerson.where('created_at >= ?', 36.hours.ago)
  puts "Found #{recent_deleted_people.count} recently deleted people."
rescue => e
  puts "An error occurred: #{e.message}"
end
```

This will output the number of `DeletedPerson` records found or an error message if something goes wrong.
